drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.
Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Rounded to nearest 0.1mg. NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed.
Take in the morning with or after food. Consider initial dose reduction in patients over 70 years of age.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-0.9 x10⁹/L, 1st occurrence, no prior G-CSF
dose_modifications.notes: Administer 100% with prophylactic growth factors. Alternative for poor PS or non-curative intent: delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then give 75% plus G-CSF.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then give 50% plus growth factors.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then give 75% plus prophylactic growth factors.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then give 50% plus growth factors.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and normal AST/ALT
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 75%. Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurotoxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine dose to 1mg. Stop vincristine for higher toxicity grades or if toxicity increases despite dose reduction.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for first cycle only (for 21 days)
Consider aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral in high-risk patients
Consider co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday in high-risk patients
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for intravenous administration of vincristine (vinca alkaloid).
Vincristine is a vesicant - extravasation risk.
Consider initial dose reduction in patients over 70 years of age.
Cyclophosphamide may cause haemorrhagic cystitis (rare). Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.
Consider gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist in high-risk patients.

drugs.notes: Available as 50mg tablets. Swallow whole with a full glass of water.
Available as 50mg and 100mg capsules. Take one hour before food or on an empty stomach. Reduce dose if significant reduction in albumin levels.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to ≥1x10⁹/L. Applies to both cyclophosphamide and etoposide.
Delay until recovery to ≥75x10⁹/L. Applies to both drugs.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction to 50%. Guidance only for palliative regimen.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 75%. Guidance only for palliative regimen.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily oral when required
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must NOT be continued beyond the prescribed days.
Patients with Hodgkin's Lymphoma carry a lifelong risk of TA-GVHD. Irradiated blood products must be used for life. Notify blood transfusion department and issue patient alert card.
Monitor albumin prior to each cycle; reduce etoposide dose if significant albumin reduction.
Patients should be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (20mg/ml).

Take in the morning with or after food. Administration begins on the morning of chemotherapy. Also acts as rituximab pre-medication. Rounded to nearest 5mg. Prednisolone 40mg/m² oral also serves as rituximab premedication.

dose_modifications.notes: Omit cyclophosphamide. Prednisolone and rituximab do not require haematological dose modifications.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >30 µmol/L and AST/ALT 2-3x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision. Evidence suggests exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary. May be decreased efficacy in severe hepatic impairment.
Reduce to 75%. Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous bolus 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Prednisolone 40mg/m² oral on morning of treatment (also chemotherapy component)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (first cycle only)
special_warnings: Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.

Rituximab associated with cytokine release syndrome (CRS) - occurs most commonly within 1-2 hours of first infusion. Monitor for severe dyspnoea, bronchospasm, hypoxia, fever, chills, urticaria, angioedema.
Rituximab associated with risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML). Monitor for neurological/cognitive/psychiatric symptoms.
Assess for cold or flu-like symptoms prior to rituximab (viral URTI increases risk of hepatotoxicity).
Consider mesna for patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.
Growth factor support recommended: filgrastim 300mcg SC daily for 7 days from day 6, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2.
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV and salbutamol 2.5mg nebule available as required for rituximab infusion reactions.
Consider pethidine 25-50mg IV for rituximab-related rigors not responding to steroids.

drugs.notes: Twice daily oral (total daily dose 200mg/m², total course dose 1000mg/m²). Available as 50mg tablets only; dose rounded to nearest 50mg. Swallow whole with a full glass of water. Drink minimum 3 litres fluid per 24 hours.
20mg IV equivalent on day 1. If rituximab is omitted, dexamethasone must be prescribed orally. On cycle 1 day 2 (split rituximab dose), hydrocortisone 100mg IV is given instead of dexamethasone.

Cycle 2 onwards: 375mg/m² in 500ml NaCl 0.9% on day 1. Cycle 1 split dose: 100mg in 50ml NaCl 0.9% over 120 min on day 1, then 325mg/m² in 500ml NaCl 0.9% on day 2 (if total IgM >20g/L). Omit rituximab if total IgM >40g/L. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L. Consider G-CSF as secondary prophylaxis. Reconsider treatment if not recovered after 14 days.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or febrile neutropenia following any cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Give G-CSF with all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia leading to infection despite G-CSF
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cyclophosphamide by 50% for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia recurs despite 50% cyclophosphamide dose reduction

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until platelets ≥100x10⁹/L. Reconsider if not recovered after 14 days.
Reduce cyclophosphamide by 50% for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 thrombocytopenia recurs despite 50% dose reduction
Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below, then reduce to 75% or discontinue as appropriate.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous if dexamethasone not given (i.e. cycle 1 day 2 split dose rituximab)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 5 days oral (take home, starting before first cyclophosphamide dose)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily (cycle 1 only, until bulk disease reduced)
special_warnings: Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Omit rituximab if total IgM >40g/L. Consider plasma exchange prior to each cycle if IgM >40g/L or clinical signs of hyperviscosity.
Split first rituximab dose if total IgM >20g/L: 100mg in 50ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours day 1, then 325mg/m² in 500ml NaCl 0.9% day 2.
Blood transfusion carries higher risk of TACO and thrombosis related to IgM paraprotein height; decisions must be made by consultant.
Rituximab associated with CRS, PML, and hypersensitivity reactions. Monitor carefully.
Regular monitoring of blood glucose is recommended (dexamethasone).
Cyclophosphamide oral: tablets must be swallowed whole, minimum 3 litres fluid per 24 hours.
Consider gastric protection with H2 antagonist or PPI.
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV and salbutamol 2.5mg nebule available as required for rituximab infusion reactions.

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.

drugs.notes: Priming dose 0.16mg on day 1. Subcutaneous injection preferably in lower abdomen or thigh. Rotate injection site left to right.
Intermediate dose 0.8mg on day 8. Subcutaneous injection.
Full dose 48mg on days 15 and 22. Day 15 first full dose MUST be administered as an in-patient with 24-hour monitoring for CRS and ICANS. Bispecific antibody CD3xCD20.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until ANC ≥0.5 x10⁹/L. No dose reductions recommended; manage with interruption or discontinuation.
Withhold until returns to <LLN-100 or baseline.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until resolved to grade 1 or baseline.

dose_modifications.condition: Re-priming required: >8 days between 0.16mg and 0.8mg doses
dose_modifications.notes: Repeat full cycle 1 priming sequence with standard CRS prophylaxis.

dose_modifications.condition: Re-priming required: >14 days between 0.8mg and 48mg doses
Re-priming required: >6 weeks between full 48mg doses

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 15mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab (all doses in cycle 1; continue for 3 consecutive days following each weekly administration)
Chlorphenamine 10mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg IV (maximum 800mg) 8-hourly if required for CRS - maximum 3 doses; one dose must be available on ward prior to each epcoritamab administration
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 28 days (TLS prophylaxis; 100mg if renal impairment)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily for 28 days
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday
special_warnings: NICE TA954 funding approval required (Blueteq form must be completed).
Epcoritamab is a bispecific antibody targeting CD3 and CD20. Step-up dosing is essential to reduce risk of CRS.
ALL patients must be hospitalised on Cycle 1 Day 15 (first 48mg dose) for 24 hours post-administration to monitor for CRS and ICANS.

CRS risk: symptoms include pyrexia, tachycardia, hypoxia, hypotension. Temperature, BP and oxygen saturation monitored 4-hourly after day 15 administration. Patient must carry CRS alert card at all times.
ICANS risk: symptoms include seizures, confusion, encephalopathy, ataxia. Seek immediate medical attention.
Tocilizumab must be prescribed in advance for CRS management. Access to additional dose within 8 hours of use must be ensured.

TLS risk assessment required prior to treatment. High-risk patients (high tumour burden and/or lymphocytes >25x10⁹/L and/or CrCl <70ml/min) require prophylaxis with rasburicase 3mg IV then allopurinol 300mg oral.
Patients with Grade ≥2 CRS or prior tocilizumab use must be hospitalised for next scheduled infusion.
Hepatitis B: check status before starting; positive serology requires liver disease expert consultation.
Viral screen required: CMV, HSV, EBV, VZV, HIV.
Symptoms of CRS can occur weeks after administration.
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >3x ULN): safety not established.
Severe renal impairment (CrCl <30ml/min): safety not established.

drugs.notes: Cycles 2-3: 48mg on days 1, 8, 15, 22 (weekly). Cycles 4-9: 48mg on days 1 and 15 (biweekly). Cycle 10 onwards: 48mg on day 1 only (monthly). Subcutaneous injection in lower abdomen or thigh. Rotate injection sites. Bispecific antibody CD3xCD20. Treatment until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until ANC ≥0.5 x10⁹/L. No dose reductions recommended.
Withhold until returns to <LLN-100 or baseline.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until resolved to grade 1 or baseline.

dose_modifications.condition: Re-priming required: >6 weeks between full 48mg doses
dose_modifications.notes: Repeat full cycle 1 priming sequence (0.16mg, 0.8mg, 48mg step-up).

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 15mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab (only for patients who experienced CRS grade 2 or 3 with previous dose; continue for 3 consecutive days following administration)
Chlorphenamine 10mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab (if prior CRS grade 2-3)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to epcoritamab (if prior CRS grade 2-3)
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg IV (maximum 800mg) available as required for CRS - maximum 3 doses
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 28 days (TLS prophylaxis)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily for 28 days
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday
special_warnings: NICE TA954 funding approval required (Blueteq form must be completed).
Up-titration to 48mg full dose must be completed before starting cycle 2 (via cycle 1 priming protocol).
Epcoritamab is a bispecific antibody targeting CD3 and CD20.
Dosing schedule changes: Cycles 2-3 weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 4-9 biweekly (days 1,15); Cycle 10+ monthly (day 1).
CRS risk: symptoms include pyrexia, tachycardia, hypoxia, hypotension. Patient must carry CRS alert card at all times. Symptoms can occur weeks after administration.
Patients who experienced Grade ≥2 CRS or received tocilizumab must be hospitalised for next scheduled infusion.
ICANS risk: symptoms include seizures, confusion, encephalopathy. Seek immediate medical attention.
Tocilizumab must be available on ward prior to each epcoritamab dose during cycles 1 and 2.
TLS risk assessment required prior to treatment.
Hepatitis B: check status before starting; positive serology requires liver disease expert consultation.
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >3x ULN): safety not established.
Severe renal impairment (CrCl <30ml/min): safety not established.
Pre-medications (dexamethasone, chlorphenamine, paracetamol) are conditional on prior CRS grade 2-3 for cycle 2 onwards.

drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.
Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then give 75%. Applies to both cyclophosphamide and fludarabine.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for first cycle only (28 days)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (continued for 6 months after treatment completion or per CD4 count)
special_warnings: Patients treated with fludarabine carry a LIFELONG risk of transfusion-associated graft versus host disease (TA-GVHD). Irradiated blood products must be used for life. Notify blood transfusion department and issue patient alert card immediately.
Direct Coombs test required prior to starting treatment.
Fludarabine patients are at higher risk of fungal and atypical infections. Maintain high level of suspicion.
Co-trimoxazole prophylaxis must continue for 6 months after treatment completion or per CD4 count.
Irradiated blood products must always be used.

drugs.notes: Once daily oral. Available as 50mg tablets; rounded to nearest 50mg. Take with breakfast, swallow whole with a full glass of water.
Once daily oral. Available as 10mg tablets; rounded to nearest 10mg. Take with lunch, swallow whole with a full glass of water. Consider switching to IV version if severe nausea/vomiting.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then give 75%. Applies to both cyclophosphamide and fludarabine.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral twice daily for 7 days (take home, starting prior to first dose of oral chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily when required
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for first cycle only (28 days)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (continued for 6 months after treatment completion or per CD4 count)
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must NOT be continued beyond prescribed days.
Patients treated with fludarabine carry a LIFELONG risk of TA-GVHD. Irradiated blood products must be used for life. Notify blood transfusion department and issue patient alert card immediately.
Direct Coombs test required prior to starting treatment.
Fludarabine patients are at higher risk of fungal and atypical infections. Maintain high level of suspicion.
Consider switching to IV version (FC IV 3-day) if severe nausea and vomiting occurs.
Patients should be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Co-trimoxazole prophylaxis must continue for 6 months after treatment completion or per CD4 count.
